# GitHub Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview

Next.js 16 App Router with TinaCMS headless CMS, Tailwind CSS 4, and Motion animation library.

### Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 16.0.7 (App Router, React 19, Turbopack)
- **CMS**: TinaCMS (Git-backed, visual editing)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui components
- **Animation**: Motion (motion/react)
- **Content**: MDX/Markdown files in `content/`

### Development Commands
```bash
pnpm dev          # Start dev server with Turbopack + TinaCMS
pnpm build        # Production build (tinacms build + next build) — also what CI runs, do not swap for build-local
pnpm build-local  # Local build without cloud checks — use to catch stale Tina types after schema changes, before pushing
```

## Architecture: Server/Client Split Pattern

Every route follows a mandatory two-file structure:
- **`page.tsx`** (server component) — fetches data via `tina/__generated__/client`, wraps content in `Layout`, and handles missing/failed data with `notFound()`.
- **`client-page.tsx`** (client component, `"use client"`) — calls `useTina({ query, data, variables })` to enable live visual editing, and renders `tinaField()` on editable elements.

```typescript
// page.tsx (server)
const data = await client.queries.post({ relativePath: `${slug}.mdx` });
return <Layout rawPageData={data}><ClientPage {...data} /></Layout>;

// client-page.tsx (client)
"use client";
const { data } = useTina({ ...props });
<h1 data-tina-field={tinaField(data.post, 'title')}>{data.post?.title}</h1>
```

## TinaCMS Schema Patterns

- Schema config lives in **`tina/config.tsx`**, which imports and registers collections.
- Each collection is its own file under `tina/collection/*.ts`, default-exported and imported into `config.tsx`.
- Custom field UIs live in `tina/fields/*.tsx` (e.g. icon picker, color picker).
- Pages are built from reusable blocks: each block's React component and its `Template` schema are exported together from the same file in `components/blocks/*.tsx`.
- New block types must be registered in the `Block` switch statement in `components/blocks/index.tsx`.
- **After editing any file in `tina/collection/` or `tina/fields/`, regenerate `tina/tina-lock.json` by running `tinacms dev --noWatch` (or `pnpm dev` briefly) before committing.** This file is only regenerated by `tinacms dev`, never by `tinacms build`. If it goes stale, CI fails with "local GraphQL schema doesn't match remote GraphQL schema" even though the content is correct — this has caused repeated CI failures on this repo. Don't try to work around it with `--skip-cloud-checks`; Tina's own docs call that "dangerous and not recommended," and it was tried and reverted here.
- A nested field sharing a name with its parent template produces doubled codegen type names (e.g. a `links` list field inside a `links` template becomes `GlobalFooterColumnsLinksLinks`). This is expected, additive codegen behavior, not a breaking schema change or a bug.

## Key File Locations

| Purpose | Location |
|---------|----------|
| TinaCMS config | `tina/config.tsx` |
| Collections | `tina/collection/*.ts` |
| Custom fields | `tina/fields/*.tsx` |
| Generated types/client | `tina/__generated__/` (auto-generated, don't edit) |
| Components | `components/` (`blocks/`, `layout/`, `ui/`, `motion-primitives/`, `magicui/`) |
| MDX rich-text components | `components/mdx-components.tsx` |
| Content | `content/{pages,posts,insights,case-studies}/*.mdx` |

## Visual Editing Rules

- Always mark editable elements with `data-tina-field={tinaField(obj, 'fieldName')}` — never a hardcoded string path.
- For blocks, apply `tinaField(block)` on the block's wrapping element.
- Render rich text with `TinaMarkdown` from `tinacms/dist/rich-text`, passing the shared `components` map from `components/mdx-components.tsx`.

## TypeScript Conventions

- Import all TinaCMS data types from `@/tina/__generated__/types` — never type CMS data as `any`.
- Type every component's props with a named `interface` (e.g. `ClientPageProps`), not inline object types.
- Client page prop interfaces typically include `query: string`, `data: <Collection>Query`, and `variables: <Collection>QueryVariables`.

## Naming Conventions

- **Files**: kebab-case (`client-page.tsx`, `mdx-components.tsx`)
- **Components/Types**: PascalCase (`ClientPage`, `PostQuery`)
- **Fields/queries**: camelCase (`postTitle`, `postConnection`)

## Error Handling

- Fetch and validate data on the server in `page.tsx`; call `notFound()` when data is missing or the fetch fails.
- Client components can assume data is valid — `useTina` only returns `data`, so don't duplicate error handling in `client-page.tsx`.

## Patterns to Avoid

- Don't call `client.queries.*` from a client component — fetching must happen server-side and be awaited.
- Don't use string literals for `data-tina-field` — always use the `tinaField()` helper.
- Don't render CMS content directly in the server component — delegate rendering to `client-page.tsx` so it stays editable.
- Don't hardcode `relativePath` — always derive it from route params.

## Before Completing a Feature

- [ ] Server component fetches via `client.queries.xxx()` and passes `{ query, data, variables }` to the client component
- [ ] Client component has `"use client"` and calls `useTina()`
- [ ] Props are typed with the generated `<Collection>Query` / `<Collection>QueryVariables` types via a named interface
- [ ] All editable elements use `tinaField()`
- [ ] Errors are handled server-side (`notFound()` on missing/failed fetch)
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes with no console errors
- [ ] If `tina/collection/` or `tina/fields/` changed: ran `pnpm build-local` to catch stale generated types, and regenerated `tina/tina-lock.json` via `tinacms dev --noWatch`
